intent: Define the SecurityRecipes browser-agent workspace boundary: a generated, machine-readable policy model for deciding when browser-based or desktop browser agents may inspect untrusted content, use logged-in sessions, hold credentials, navigate origins, fill forms, download files, call local services, or deliver outputs through external routes.

positioning.buyer: AI Platform, Product Security, Browser Platform, Security Engineering, SOC, GRC, procurement security, and agent-host teams
positioning.category: Browser-agent workspace isolation and ambient-authority control for agentic AI
positioning.enterprise_promise: SecurityRecipes can make browser agents easier to approve by turning prompt-injection exposure, logged-in session state, local credential storage, origin changes, file downloads, localhost access, and side effects into deterministic allow, hold, deny, or kill decisions.
positioning.why_now: Agentic AI is moving from chat and code review into browsers, email, documents, web apps, and desktop workspaces. Browser agents combine untrusted content with ambient user sessions, DOM-visible data, local storage, downloads, forms, and external communication, so enterprises need a boundary that constrains impact even when prompt injection or social-engineering attacks are not perfectly detected.

standards_alignment.name: OpenAI: Designing AI agents to resist prompt injection
standards_alignment.coverage: Treats prompt injection as a social-engineering style agent-security problem and emphasizes constraining dangerous sources-to-sinks combinations such as untrusted content plus external transmission or tool actions.

standards_alignment.name: OpenAI: Continuously hardening ChatGPT Atlas against prompt injection attacks
standards_alignment.coverage: Frames browser agents as high-value targets because they view pages, click, type, and act in the same browser spaces, contexts, and data surfaces users rely on.

standards_alignment.name: Anthropic: Mitigating the risk of prompt injections in browser use
standards_alignment.coverage: Documents browser-specific prompt-injection risk from webpages, embedded documents, ads, dynamic scripts, forms, clicks, downloads, and email-like workflows that can expose confidential data.

standards_alignment.name: Microsoft: Experimental Agentic Features and Agent Workspace
standards_alignment.coverage: Supports dedicated agent accounts, contained workspaces, scoped authorization, runtime isolation, visibility, and user control for agentic desktop/browser-style automation.

standards_alignment.name: CAISI Request for Information on Securing AI Agent Systems
standards_alignment.coverage: Calls out indirect prompt injection, data poisoning, misaligned actions, and deployment interventions that constrain and monitor agent access in real environments.

standards_alignment.name: NIST AI Agent Standards Initiative
standards_alignment.coverage: Aligns browser-agent boundaries to secure and interoperable agent adoption, agent identity, authorization, and evaluations for agents that operate across digital ecosystems.

standards_alignment.name: Model Context Protocol Authorization Specification
standards_alignment.coverage: Connects browser-agent tool handoffs to resource indicators, token audience binding, PKCE, short-lived tokens, secure token storage, and token-passthrough denial.

standards_alignment.name: OWASP Top 10 for Agentic Applications 2026
standards_alignment.coverage: Maps browser-agent controls to goal hijacking, tool misuse, identity and privilege abuse, memory/context poisoning, cascading failures, and rogue-agent containment.

workspace_classes.title: Public research browser
workspace_classes.description: Logged-out browser workspace for reading public web pages and collecting cited security references.
workspace_classes.commercial_value: Safe public research is the low-friction entry point for hosted browser-agent context collection.

workspace_classes.title: SecurityRecipes browser planner
workspace_classes.description: Browser-side planner that gathers page, recipe, GitHub, and dependency context, then emits draft-first outputs through user-selected delivery routes.
prohibited_conditions: provider or GitHub tokens copied into prompts
Slack, Jira, email, or GitHub writes without explicit route selection
execution against shared or untrusted browser profiles
workspace_classes.commercial_value: Turns the existing beta planner into a governed front-end contract for a future hosted scheduler.

workspace_classes.title: Enterprise isolated browser workspace
workspace_classes.description: Dedicated agent account and isolated browser or desktop session with scoped files, scoped apps, controlled origins, and auditable user approvals.
workspace_classes.commercial_value: This is the enterprise default for browser agents: useful enough for workflows, bounded enough for procurement.

workspace_classes.title: Email and document browser agent
workspace_classes.description: Agent workflow that reads mail, documents, calendars, tickets, or shared-drive pages where attacker-controlled content may impersonate authority.
workspace_classes.commercial_value: Email and document agents are high-demand but high-risk; this pack gives buyers a concrete approval gate.

workspace_classes.title: Logged-in personal browser
workspace_classes.description: A user's normal browser profile with ambient cookies, password managers, extensions, synced tabs, and uncontrolled local storage.
workspace_classes.commercial_value: Clear denial of ambient user authority is a trust-builder for enterprise browser-agent adoption.

workspace_classes.title: Localhost and developer tooling browser
workspace_classes.description: Browser agent that can reach localhost, loopback services, dev servers, notebook workspaces, IDE bridges, or local agent control ports.
prohibited_conditions: untrusted web page can trigger localhost navigation
devtools or notebook endpoint can execute code
browser can bridge public web content to loopback APIs
workspace_classes.commercial_value: Prevents browser agents from becoming bridges into local MCP, notebooks, IDEs, and developer machines.

workspace_classes.title: Payment or admin console browser
workspace_classes.description: Browser agent reaches money movement, account administration, production settings, IAM, customer data exports, or destructive consoles.
workspace_classes.commercial_value: Board-grade proof that the product does not silently automate irreversible browser actions.

task_profiles.title: Public security research
task_profiles.description: Read public standards, vendor advisories, public GitHub metadata, and public documentation for cited context.

task_profiles.title: Draft remediation handoff
task_profiles.description: Generate a draft PR packet, issue body, email, Slack message, Jira ticket, or runbook receipt after user review.

task_profiles.title: Email and document triage
task_profiles.description: Read and summarize messages, documents, or tickets while treating every embedded instruction as untrusted content.

task_profiles.title: Internal webapp form fill
task_profiles.description: Fill bounded forms in approved internal apps with explicit confirmation before submit.

task_profiles.title: Localhost developer tool review
task_profiles.description: Inspect local dev pages only when loopback routes are explicitly allowlisted and public web pages cannot initiate them.

task_profiles.title: Admin or payment observation
task_profiles.description: Read-only observation of high-impact consoles for drafting instructions, never autonomous submission.

commercialization_path.open_layer: Public browser-agent boundary model, deterministic generator, runtime evaluator, generated JSON evidence pack, and docs that make browser-agent safety concrete.
commercialization_path.team_layer: Custom origin policies, delivery-route policy, browser storage policy, localhost allowlists, and customer-specific browser task manifests.
commercialization_path.enterprise_layer: Hosted browser-agent policy API, dedicated agent workspace broker, browser isolation adapters, prompt-injection event ingestion, signed run receipts, and SIEM/SOAR export.
commercialization_path.acquirer_value: A frontier lab, AI browser vendor, security platform, or developer tooling company gets a ready-made boundary for the hardest agent adoption surface: logged-in browsers and untrusted web content.

enterprise_adoption_packet.board_level_claim: SecurityRecipes can decide whether a browser agent should proceed before untrusted content meets logged-in sessions, local storage, downloads, localhost, forms, or external delivery routes.
default_questions_answered: Can this browser task run logged out?
Is this a dedicated agent profile or a user's normal browser?
Can untrusted content influence an external send or form submit?
Are provider, GitHub, session, or customer tokens visible to the page or model?
Is localhost, download, admin-console, or payment authority reachable?
Which telemetry and receipt fields prove what happened?
enterprise_adoption_packet.recommended_first_use: Attach this pack to browser-agent launches, AI browser procurement, desktop agent workspace design, beta planner approvals, and quarterly prompt-injection tabletop exercises.
enterprise_adoption_packet.sales_motion: Lead with a readable browser-agent safety gate, then sell hosted policy, dedicated workspace brokering, origin enforcement, prompt-injection telemetry, and signed browser run receipts.
